title: continue should be avoided
description: continue should not be used. continue is an unstructured control flow statement that makes code less testable, less readable and less maintainable. Structured control flow statements such as if should be used instead.
recommendation: Replace continue with structured control flow using if statements.

noncompliant: for (i = 0; i < 10; i++) { if (i == 5) { continue; } alert('i = ' + i); }

checks.message: continue statement should be avoided. Use structured control flow instead.
labeled continue statement should be avoided. Use structured control flow instead.
